palette_hint: Background #F4F0E8 (aged newsprint), primary text #1A1A1A, accent 1 #C8392B (alert red), accent 2 #2E5D8B (deep ocean blue); section dividers use #D9D3C4.
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif at heavy weight for authoritative gravitas; body in system-ui sans-serif at 400/16px for legibility; data labels in monospace (Courier New) for numerical precision.
description: A multi-page investigative special report by the Tethys Journalism Collective documenting five years of illegal deep-sea trawling in the South Coral Sea, combining data visualizations, field correspondent logs, and policy analysis.
constraints: Each page must contain at least 3 visually distinct layout regions: a full-width header band (max 100vw, 80px tall), a primary content grid (max-width 1160px, centered, 48px horizontal padding), and a footer rail (background #1A1A1A, text #F4F0E8, 32px vertical padding).
Typographic scale: report titles at 48px Georgia bold, section headers at 28px Georgia italic, sub-labels at 11px Courier New uppercase letter-spacing 0.12em, body paragraphs at 16px/1.7 system-ui.
Spacing system: base unit 8px; section gaps 64px; card internal padding 24px; column gutters 24px; no section may have vertical padding less than 40px.
Accent red #C8392B used exclusively for alert callouts, pull-quote borders (4px left border), and critical statistic highlights — never as a background fill larger than 8px.
Mixed layout mandate: each page must use CSS Grid for the main content region, Flexbox for navigation and card rows, and at least one absolutely-positioned annotation or label element per page.

page_specs.overview: The entry page features a full-bleed header band with the report title 'Operation Phantom Net: Five Years of Silence in the South Coral Sea' in 48px Georgia bold on #2E5D8B background with white text, followed by a 3-column CSS Grid summary section containing: a 400-word executive summary, a sidebar of 6 key statistics (vessel count 847, km² of seabed damaged 12,400, tonnes of bycatch 94,000, nations implicated 11, prosecutions 3, fines levied $220,000 USD) each styled in Courier New monospace with red accent underlines, and a vertical timeline strip of 5 major events from 2019–2024. A full-width pull-quote in 28px Georgia italic with a 4px #C8392B left border separates the grid from a 2-column 'How We Investigated' methodology note at the bottom.
page_specs.data-atlas: A data-dense page organized as a CSS Grid with a 2/3 + 1/3 column split at 1160px max-width: the left panel contains six labeled 'incident zone' boxes for the six primary fishing grounds (Challenger Deep Shelf, Rowley Shoals Corridor, Louisiade Plateau, Marion Reef Zone, Osprey Reef Approach, Holmes Reef Perimeter), each a bordered card (border 1px #D9D3C4, 24px padding) with coordinates, vessel incident count, and a horizontal CSS bar chart built from div widths showing incident density by year 2019–2024. The right panel holds an absolutely-positioned 'live ticker' label and a stacked column of 8 named vessel profiles (e.g., MV Shenlong 7, FV Maris Stella II) with flag emoji, tonnage, and last-known port. A full-width monospace data table of 12 rows at the bottom lists annual catch estimates by species group with alternating row backgrounds #F4F0E8 and #E8E2D6.
page_specs.field-dispatches: Three correspondent field reports presented as a magazine-style irregular grid: the first dispatch ('Rabaul, PNG — March 2023') occupies a 2-column-wide card with a large pull-quote, a 300-word narrative, and an absolutely-positioned dateline stamp in 11px Courier New uppercase. The second dispatch ('Cairns, QLD — August 2023') sits in a single narrow column card with a red-accented sidebar stat block listing 3 intercepted radio frequencies and 2 vessel names. The third dispatch ('Manila, PHL — January 2024') spans full width with a 2-column internal layout splitting the narrative from a 6-item bulleted evidence log. Each card uses Flexbox internally, has a top border in #2E5D8B 3px, and 40px vertical padding; a 64px gap separates all three cards.
page_specs.policy-matrix: A policy analysis page structured in three horizontal bands: the top band is a 4-column Flexbox row of 'jurisdiction cards' (UNCLOS, WCPFC, Australian AFMA, Papua New Guinea NFA) each 24px padded with a header in 11px Courier New and 2–3 sentence assessment in 16px system-ui, with a color-coded status badge (red = non-compliant, blue = partial, dark = compliant) using a 6px wide left border. The middle band is a full-width CSS Grid comparison table with 5 columns (Jurisdiction, Patrol Frequency, Penalty Cap, Prosecutions 2019–2024, Recommended Reform) and 6 data rows, alternating background #F4F0E8/#E8E2D6, headers in Courier New uppercase. The bottom band presents 3 recommended policy reforms as large-type numbered items (01, 02, 03) in 28px Georgia italic with 200-word supporting paragraphs, separated by 1px #D9D3C4 horizontal rules.
page_specs.about-the-investigation: A narrative-focused page with a single 680px-wide centered text column for the main 500-word methodology and ethics statement, flanked by two absolutely-positioned marginal annotation blocks (left: funding disclosure list, right: data sources cited — 7 sources) in 11px Courier New on #E8E2D6 background, each 200px wide. Below the centered column, a Flexbox row of 5 journalist profile cards (each 180px wide, 24px padding, 1px #D9D3C4 border) displays name, region, and a 40-word bio for Mara Soliven, Declan Thorpe, Yuki Tanamoto, Amara Osei, and Petra Vujić. A final full-width dark band (#1A1A1A) above the footer contains the report's editorial independence statement in centered 16px system-ui white text and the Tethys Journalism Collective masthead in 28px Georgia white.
